text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr A. Patel
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr A. Patel
Consultant Neurologist,  

Samuel Green, DOB: 14-03-1986, Hospital No: KCH723518 NHS No. 9983214576  
12 Brookfield Road, London, SE15 3QH

Thank you for asking me to review this patient in our epilepsy clinic. He has a remote history of mild traumatic brain injury in his twenties without documented loss of consciousness beyond a few minutes and no neurosurgical intervention at that time. He reports that his first seizures occurred several years later and that, over time, his events have evolved with a clear focal onset. Yesterday he experienced three tonic-clonic seizures yesterday, each preceded by a stereotyped warning of a rising epigastric sensation, brief déjà vu, and impaired awareness with right-hand automatisms lasting around 30–60 seconds before secondary generalisation. His partner confirms post-ictal confusion for 20–30 minutes after each event.

On current history and semiology, this is most consistent with focal epilepsy. He describes interictal brief auras occurring approximately once or twice per week without progression, and nocturnal events are rare. Known triggers include sleep deprivation and missed meals; he denies alcohol excess. There is no family history of epilepsy. He has noted occasional word-finding difficulty for several hours after larger seizures but no persistent focal deficits.

Medication history: He is taking lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and levetiracetam 1 g twice daily with good adherence by report; no significant adverse effects beyond mild irritability on levetiracetam which he feels is manageable. He has not previously tried lacosamide or brivaracetam. No known drug allergies.

Examination today between events was unremarkable with normal cranial nerves, no lateralising motor signs, intact coordination, and normal gait. Cardiovascular and general examination were also normal.

Prior investigations: A routine EEG from 2023 was reported as showing intermittent left temporal sharp waves. Brain MRI (epilepsy protocol) from the same year described subtle left mesial temporal T2/FLAIR signal without volume loss; no acute lesions. Basic bloods (FBC, U&Es, LFTs) in June 2025 were within normal limits.

Plan discussed with the patient: Given the focal semiology with secondary generalisation and the recent clustering of three tonic-clonic seizures yesterday, I have recommended an urgent ambulatory EEG with sleep to capture interictal activity and to reassess localisation, along with repeat MRI using an updated temporal lobe protocol. We will also check lamotrigine and levetiracetam trough levels and standard bloods. He is keen to continue current treatment for now; we discussed potential adjustment to introduce lacosamide as add-on if further generalised seizures occur pending results. He will maintain a detailed seizure diary including auras, precipitating factors, and recovery times. Safety advice regarding driving, bathing, and machinery has been reiterated in accordance with current DVLA guidance.

I will review him in six weeks or sooner if there is any escalation. Please let me know if there are any concerns in the interim.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Yesterday he experienced three tonic-clonic seizures yesterday

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure frequency statements. The letter states: 'Yesterday he experienced three tonic-clonic seizures yesterday...' This provides a clear numeric count within a day. There are also 'interictal brief auras occurring approximately once or twice per week' but these are auras without progression; per rule 7, select the highest frequency across seizure types. Three in one day is higher than 1 to 2 per week. Therefore, the current seizure frequency is 3 per day. No other conflicting daily rates are provided. Not seizure free.
seizure_frequency_number: Yesterday he experienced three tonic-clonic seizures yesterday
